description: Golden set for the data-table extraction eval. Values hand-verified against the PDFs in seed_data/. 'primitive' columns are stated verbatim in the paper; 'derived' columns require arithmetic and are never stated as a number in the paper.

papers.title: Chain-of-Thought Prompting Elicits Reasoning in Large Language Models

columns.label: GSM8K solve rate of PaLM 540B with standard prompting (%)
columns.source: Table 1, p.20 (17.9); Figure 2, p.2 rounds to 18

columns.label: GSM8K solve rate of PaLM 540B with chain-of-thought prompting (%)
columns.source: Table 1, p.20 (56.9); Figure 2, p.2 rounds to 57

columns.label: Sports understanding accuracy of PaLM 540B with chain-of-thought prompting (%)
columns.source: p.7 prose: 'outperforming an unaided sports enthusiast on sports understanding (95.4% vs 84%)'

columns.label: Sports understanding accuracy of the unaided sports enthusiast human baseline (%)
Ratio of PaLM 540B GSM8K solve rate with chain-of-thought prompting to standard prompting
columns.note: Paper says only 'performance more than doubled'; ratio never stated. Table 1 DOES print the additive delta (+39.0), which is why the delta is not usable as a derived column.

inputs.cot: GSM8K solve rate of PaLM 540B with chain-of-thought prompting (%)
inputs.std: GSM8K solve rate of PaLM 540B with standard prompting (%)

columns.label: Percentage points by which PaLM 540B with chain-of-thought prompting outperforms the unaided sports enthusiast on sports understanding
columns.note: Both operands in one sentence on p.7; difference never stated.

inputs.cot_sports: Sports understanding accuracy of PaLM 540B with chain-of-thought prompting (%)
inputs.human_sports: Sports understanding accuracy of the unaided sports enthusiast human baseline (%)

papers.title: Public use of a generalist LLM chatbot for health queries

columns.label: Share of mobile conversations classified as Symptom Questions and Health Concerns (%)
columns.source: p.3: '15.9%, 95% CI 15.8-16.0, of conversations versus 6.9% ... on desktop'

columns.label: Share of desktop conversations classified as Symptom Questions and Health Concerns (%)
Share of desktop conversations classified as Medical Paperwork (%)
columns.source: p.3: "'Medical Paperwork' is 15.7%, 95% CI 15.6-15.8, versus 2.7%, 95% CI 2.7-2.8"

columns.label: Share of mobile conversations classified as Medical Paperwork (%)
Difference in percentage points between mobile and desktop share of Symptom Questions and Health Concerns conversations

inputs.mobile: Share of mobile conversations classified as Symptom Questions and Health Concerns (%)
inputs.desktop: Share of desktop conversations classified as Symptom Questions and Health Concerns (%)

columns.label: Ratio of desktop to mobile share of Medical Paperwork conversations

inputs.desktop_mp: Share of desktop conversations classified as Medical Paperwork (%)
inputs.mobile_mp: Share of mobile conversations classified as Medical Paperwork (%)

papers.title: Finding deceivers in social context with large language models: the case of the Mafia game

columns.source: Table 4, p.6, Total row; also p.4 prose

columns.label: Total single-match accuracy of GPT-4 without voting information (%)
columns.source: Table 7, p.9, 'w/o vote' column, single-match Total row

columns.label: Total single-match accuracy of GPT-3.5-turbo at identifying mafia players (%)
columns.source: Table 4, p.6, Total row; also p.4 prose

columns.label: Percentage-point drop in GPT-4 total single-match accuracy when voting information is removed

inputs.novote: Total single-match accuracy of GPT-4 without voting information (%)
